clinical_history: ECG from a 30-year old woman shows sinus rhythm and which ONE of the following?
diagnosis: This ECG is entirely within normal limits. The rhythm is sinus at a rate of about 70 bpm. The basic intervals are as follows:  PR 0.13s; QRS duration 0.07s, and QT interval 0.38s--all within normal limits. The mean QRS axis is about +30 degrees. The P wave morphology and duration are normal. There is no evidence of left or right ventricular hypertrophy. R wave progression is normal in the chest leads and the T wave axis in the frontal and horizontal planes is normal. ST segments are normal. Note is made that a normal resting (or even exercise) ECG does not exclude underlying clinically important cardiac pathology or intermittent arrhythmias.
